acceptance.assertion: Friction classification distinguishes connector transport 502/stream exceptions from runtime policy gates, command return codes, platform filters and repository failures.
A documented or typed probe captures operator/tunnel service state, recent streamable_http errors, request concurrency hints and adjacent successful calls using bounded, redacted output.
Operator docs define when to split broad terminal calls into smaller typed calls, when to retry once, and when to stop and record friction instead of looping.
A successful retry never proves the first failure was harmless; status output preserves non-claims and recommends follow-up only when recurrence threshold is met.
Focused tests cover classification of 502-like connector symptoms, timeout symptoms, adjacent successful retry, and non-confusion with fail-closed recovery gates.

goal: Make intermittent ChatGPT-to-Grabowski connector 502 and stream/timeout failures diagnosable and less likely by adding a bounded transport-failure classification, narrow probe guidance and retry/splitting rules without treating retries as proof of correctness.

metadata.live_basis: grabowski-operator.service and tunnel-client-grabowski.service were active with zero restarts; journal near the event showed streamable_http POST handling exceptions and many concurrent forwarded commands.
observed_symptoms: grabowski_terminal_run broad combined grep/find command returned 502 upstream/external service error while a smaller follow-up call succeeded.
grabowski_recovery_status initially timed out and then succeeded on retry with all recovery checks green.

validated_acceptance.bounded-probes: PR #124 friction_summary exposes connector_transport_diagnostics with recommended bounded probes for grabowski_status, service status, recent MCP stream/journal lines and one adjacent small typed read-only call.
validated_acceptance.no-false-green: PR #124 diagnostics explicitly state a successful retry does not prove the first failure harmless and does_not_establish includes transport_reliability_proof and safe_mutation_retry.
validated_acceptance.split-retry-policy: PR #124 documents and emits split_retry_policy: read-only retry limit 1, split broad terminal calls, no mutation retry until target state is re-read, record friction when path changes.
validated_acceptance.tests: make validate passed after PR #124 implementation; focused tests in tests/test_friction.py cover connector_transport diagnostics, retry policy and separation from recovery timeout.
validated_acceptance.transport-failure-class: PR #124 adds connector_transport to the event schema, FRICTION_KINDS and FAILURE_CLASSES; tests verify 502 connector symptoms classify as connector_transport while recovery timeout/operator_bug remains actionable_failure.

validation.grabowski_ci: GitHub validate (3.10) and validate (3.12) succeeded before merge.
validation.grabowski_local: make validate passed: 710 tests OK, operator context current, policy checks passed, no obvious committed secrets detected.
validation.review_gate: tools/pr_review_gate.py PASS with self-review bound to head f97143a8afac81cb36adf4e1cbaecb4938b0824e and diff 02056c7b57fe276ffa090c42b70f6e61fba3ef3049e756cb0e86c96977842271 plus agy external review PASS.

metadata.verification_note: Grabowski PR #124 merged. It adds connector_transport as a first-class friction kind/failure class, separates 502/MCP stream/connector timeout symptoms from policy gates and recovery bugs, adds read-only connector_transport_diagnostics with bounded probe and split/retry policy, updates docs and regenerated operator context.

title: Harden connector 502 transport observability
